response.justification: Szabo is an exceptionally talented full-stack engineer with proven software craftsmanship, complex systems experience, and even prior IDE tooling work. However, the role explicitly requires deep Java, Kotlin, and Spring ecosystem expertise—areas where his resume shows minimal recent experience. His 15+ year career is heavily weighted toward TypeScript/JavaScript and D, with Java work scattered and dormant since 2018. While his architectural thinking could eventually translate, the steep learning curve on the core technology stack would significantly delay productivity in a mid-senior individual contributor role expecting immediate impact.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship with TDD, CI/CD, clean architecture across multiple complex systems (microservices at scale, real-time data handling)
Direct IDE tooling experience at National Instruments on LabWindows/CVI (C IDE) showing understanding of language tools and developer-facing systems
Exceptional ability to master diverse tech stacks and work across full stack, as evidenced by proficiency spanning Node.js, D, C/C++, and infrastructure
concerns: Minimal Java expertise—only scattered mentions across 15+ year career; last significant Java work in 2018; zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise framework knowledge
Career trajectory heavily favors JavaScript/TypeScript and D rather than JVM ecosystem; no demonstrated depth in Kotlin, build tools, or enterprise frameworks
IDE/language tooling experience is outdated (2014-2015); no recent work with modern IDE architectures, language servers, LSP, compiler integration, or contemporary tooling patterns

key_factors.factor: Spring ecosystem and enterprise framework knowledge
Software engineering fundamentals and architectural thinking
